title: Dark Memory MCP v1 — persistent memory + workflow orchestration as a sibling of dark-research-mcp

intent.what: Build Dark Memory MCP, a standalone Go module and MCP server, that owns the persistent memory and workflow orchestration layer for dark-agents-v2. It is a SIBLING of dark-research-mcp (the OSINT router), not a refactor or replacement. Both tools install independently via opencode.jsonc and coexist. dark-research-mcp's internal `dark_mem_*` namespace is marked DEPRECATED and emits warnings, but stays functional for users who haven't migrated yet. Dark Memory MCP exposes ~52 tools in the `dark_memory_*` namespace, organized by lifecycle: session, research, vibeflow, ssd, constitution, mods, economy, observability, admin. Dual-driver persistence (SQLite + Postgres) via a single Store interface. Six operational invariants baked into every operation (write audit, per-session scoping, canary in writes, constitution audit, cache integrity, mod content sanitization).
intent.why: Three forces converge: (1) SQLite's single-writer mutex is the operational ceiling under multi-agent load — Postgres with proper connection pooling removes it. (2) dark-mem currently lives as `internal/mem/` inside dark-research-mcp, which couples memory to the OSINT router and makes it impossible to evolve independently or share with other agents. (3) The dark-recall v2 plugin auto-prefills recalled research into every LLM turn, and the existing schema does not distinguish cross-session reads from per-session reads — an architecture-level gap, not a CVE. Refactoring internal/mem/ would force every existing dark-research-mcp user through a migration. Building Dark Memory MCP as a sibling with parallel install is the cleaner product move: users opt in, dark-research-mcp's internal tools stay (deprecated), no forced upgrade.
non_goals: MySQL or other drivers (v1 supports SQLite + Postgres only)

dark_research_mcp.deprecated_namespace: dark_mem_* (emits deprecation warning on every call)
dark_research_mcp.binary_status: stable; no major changes planned; deprecated code frozen

dark_memory_mcp.binary_status: active development; v1.0 ships this spec

coexistence_model.opencode.jsonc_layout: Both MCP servers registered side by side. The dark-recall plugin v2 prefers dark_memory_* calls when the dark-memory-mcp server is detected. Falls back to dark_mem_* with a one-time toast otherwise.
coexistence_model.migration_strategy: opt-in. No forced upgrade. Users add dark-memory-mcp to opencode.jsonc and update their LLM tool preferences at their own pace. The deprecation shim in dark-research-mcp runs indefinitely with no maintenance commitment.

must_have: go.mod declares module github.com/dark-agents/dark-memory-mcp with NO mcp-go dependency
internal/store.Store interface exists; both internal/store/sqlite and internal/store/postgres implement it
go test ./tests/dual_driver/... passes against BOTH drivers with identical assertions
dark-memory-mcp binary starts, registers all dark_memory_* tools, and survives a 1000-call stress test
dark-memory-cli binary supports: migrate, vacuum, schema-status, set-driver
Write audit row emitted for every Save* call (verify in tests/audit_test.go)
Canary check rejects Save* whose payload contains the active canary (verify in tests/canary_test.go)
Per-session Recall filter returns correct subset (verify in tests/session_scope_test.go)
Constitution watchdog raises on SHA256 mismatch (verify in tests/watchdog_test.go)
All 6 invariants from the constitution exercised by at least one test
should_have: Migrations v1–v6 applied cleanly to a fresh dark.db (SQLite)
Migrations v1–v6 applied cleanly to a fresh Postgres database
Existing dark.db (v3 schema) migrates forward to v6 without data loss
Performance: P50 SaveRun latency < 5ms on SQLite, < 10ms on Postgres (LAN)
Performance: P50 Recall latency < 10ms on SQLite with 10k rows, < 30ms on Postgres with 100k rows
dark-research-mcp's internal dark_mem_* tools emit deprecation warning + recommend Dark Memory MCP
could_have: Tool: dark_memory_memory_state returns the LLM-visible memory footprint
Vector recall via pgvector extension (Postgres-only, optional)

evidence_required: go test ./... — passes on both drivers
go build ./... — produces dark-memory-mcp.exe + dark-memory-cli.exe + dark-memory-inspect.exe
End-to-end: dark-memory-mcp starts, registers 52 tools, accepts 1000 calls without deadlock
Drift verdict: dark_ssd_drift_judge applied to the generated artifacts vs this spec
